units.name: Fix cost_source attribution and Copilot pricing
units.description: R5+R6: Change cost_source from 'calculated' to 'estimated' when using _estimate_tokens() heuristic. Add COPILOT_PRICING dict with GPT-4o rates, replace CLAUDE_PRICING lookup for Copilot. Add _PRICING_LAST_VERIFIED date constant. Warn in reports if pricing >90 days old.
acceptance: NdjsonStreamCollector sets cost_source='estimated' (not 'calculated') when _estimate_tokens() heuristic is used — verify in test_telemetry.py
COPILOT_PRICING dict exists with GPT-4o input/output rates separate from CLAUDE_PRICING
NdjsonStreamCollector uses COPILOT_PRICING (not CLAUDE_PRICING) for Copilot cost calculation
_PRICING_LAST_VERIFIED date constant exists next to pricing dicts
All existing tests pass: python -m pytest tests/test_telemetry.py -v

units.name: Git reset between sequential tasks
units.description: R2: After each task completes in sequential mode (parallel=1), run git checkout -- . && git clean -fd to reset the working directory. Prevents task N's leftover files from corrupting task N+1's results.
acceptance: executor.py contains git reset logic (git checkout -- . && git clean -fd) that runs between sequential tasks
Reset only runs when parallel <= 1 (sequential mode), not in parallel mode
New test in test_executor.py verifies reset subprocess call occurs between tasks
All existing tests pass: python -m pytest tests/test_executor.py -v

units.name: Deprecate unused token_count field
units.description: R14: Remove token_count field from AgentOutput and CompletedTask. No adapter sets this field (always None). It is replaced by input_tokens + output_tokens.
acceptance: AgentOutput dataclass/model no longer has a token_count field
CompletedTask dataclass/model no longer has a token_count field
No references to token_count remain in adapters/ or models/ (grep returns empty)
All existing tests pass: python -m pytest tests/ -v --timeout=30

units.name: Subprocess env whitelist and scoring symlinks fix
units.description: P0 premortem mitigations: (1) Add explicit env whitelist to subprocess.run() in _base.py using a safe_env() approach — only pass necessary vars, not full parent env. (2) Change symlinks=True to symlinks=False in scoring.py copytree. (3) Fix MCP temp file lifecycle — use context manager with finally cleanup so secrets don't persist on crash.
acceptance: _base.py subprocess.run() calls pass an explicit env parameter (not inheriting full parent env)
The env whitelist includes PATH, HOME, and agent-specific vars (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GITHUB_TOKEN etc.) but NOT all parent env vars
scoring.py shutil.copytree uses symlinks=False (not True)
MCP temp files are cleaned up in a finally block or context manager, not just happy path
All existing tests pass: python -m pytest tests/test_scoring.py tests/test_adapters.py -v

units.name: Update interpret SKILL.md to match reality
units.description: Fix documentation-implementation gap: SKILL.md promises Cohen's d, confidence intervals, per-task tables, and HTML reports that don't exist. Update to accurately describe current capabilities. Note planned features as 'Coming soon' rather than describing them as available.
acceptance: SKILL.md does not claim Cohen's d, confidence intervals, or HTML reports exist as current features
SKILL.md accurately describes current output formats (text, json)
Planned features (CIs, HTML, CSV, per-task tables) are listed under a 'Planned' or 'Coming soon' section

units.name: Statistical hypothesis testing and confidence intervals
units.description: R4: Add McNemar's exact test for binary pass/fail, Wilcoxon signed-rank for continuous scores, Cliff's delta effect size for binary, Cohen's d for continuous. Wilson score intervals for pass rates on ConfigSummary. Add billing_model field to ConfigSummary. Sample-size warning when N < 10. Pin scipy>=1.11,<2 in pyproject.toml.
acceptance: PairwiseComparison dataclass has fields: p_value (float|None), effect_size (float|None), effect_size_method (str), ci_lower (float), ci_upper (float)
ConfigSummary has fields: ci_lower (float), ci_upper (float), billing_model (str)
McNemar's exact test implemented (~15 lines, no scipy needed) and used for binary outcomes
Wilcoxon signed-rank test used for continuous scores via scipy.stats.wilcoxon
Cliff's delta computed for binary data, Cohen's d for continuous data
Wilson score interval computed for pass rates
Sample-size warning field populated when N < 10 tasks
scipy>=1.11,<2 pinned in pyproject.toml
New tests in test_analysis.py verify all statistical functions with known inputs/outputs
python -m pytest tests/test_analysis.py -v passes

units.name: Git worktree isolation for parallel execution
units.description: R1 + P0 concurrency: Create IsolationStrategy protocol in core/isolation.py with WorktreeIsolation default. Pool of N worktrees matching --parallel count. Reset worktree between tasks. Rewrite prompt repo_path to worktree path in preamble.py. Add isolate_session() to AgentAdapter protocol for per-adapter session dir isolation (CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR etc). Add global concurrency semaphore capping total active subprocesses.
acceptance: core/isolation.py exists with IsolationStrategy protocol and WorktreeIsolation class
WorktreeIsolation creates git worktrees via subprocess, manages a pool of N worktrees
WorktreeIsolation.reset() runs git checkout -- . && git clean -fd in the worktree
preamble.py repo_path is rewritten to worktree path when isolation is active
AgentAdapter protocol includes isolate_session() method signature
ClaudeAdapter.isolate_session() sets CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR to a per-slot temp directory
Global concurrency semaphore exists in executor.py capping total subprocess count
Executor uses isolation strategy when parallel > 1
New tests verify worktree creation, cleanup, path rewriting, and session isolation
python -m pytest tests/test_executor.py tests/test_adapters.py -v passes

units.name: Repeat run infrastructure
units.description: R3: Add repeat_index field to CompletedTask. Add --repeats N CLI flag to run_cmd.py. Executor runs each task N times per config. Results stored with repeat index. Checkpoint tracks repeats. Interpret aggregates across repeats.
acceptance: CompletedTask has repeat_index: int field (default 0)
run_cmd.py accepts --repeats N argument (default 1)
Executor executes each task N times when --repeats N is specified
Checkpoint store distinguishes repeat runs (same task_id, different repeat_index)
interpret_cmd.py aggregates scores across repeats for the same task_id
python -m pytest tests/test_executor.py tests/test_checkpoint.py tests/test_models.py -v passes

units.name: Per-task breakdown and CSV export
units.description: R7: Add CSV export format and per-task breakdown to reports. CSV columns: config, task_id, repeat, score, pass, duration_sec, cost_usd, cost_source, input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_read_tokens, cost_model. Include nullable columns for future stats (ci_lower, ci_upper). Text report includes per-task table. Add --format csv to interpret CLI.
acceptance: interpret_cmd.py accepts --format csv and produces CSV output
CSV has columns: config, task_id, repeat, score, pass, duration_sec, cost_usd, cost_source, input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_read_tokens, cost_model, ci_lower, ci_upper
Text report includes per-task breakdown table (not just aggregates)
JSON report includes full per-task data array
CSV header includes warning comment when repeats=1: '# SINGLE RUN — no statistical confidence'
python -m pytest tests/test_analysis.py tests/test_experiment_cmd.py -v passes

units.name: Self-contained HTML report
units.description: R8: Add format_html_report() producing a single self-contained .html file with inline CSS/JS. Includes: executive summary, ranking table, per-task drill-down, pairwise comparison cards, cost-efficiency section separating per-token from subscription agents. Suppress CI bars when repeats=1 (show point estimates with banner instead).
acceptance: interpret_cmd.py accepts --format html and produces a .html file
HTML file is self-contained (inline CSS/JS, no external dependencies)
HTML includes executive summary section with headline recommendation
HTML includes ranking table with scores and costs
HTML includes per-task drill-down section
HTML includes pairwise comparison cards
HTML separates per-token and subscription agents in cost section
When repeats=1, CI bars are NOT shown — point estimates with 'Single run' banner instead
python -m pytest tests/test_analysis.py -v passes
